message.content:
You are a DIRTY-ROOM (open-side) diagnosis agent for the tamarin-rs sealed relicensing campaign. You are BOTH-SIDES by role: you MAY read the GPL Haskell source AND the sealed workspace. Your output feeds the sealed room only through a filtered behavioral-instruction block, so precision about what goes in that block is critical. CONTEXT. The sealed pretty round-6 implementer built the web rendering mode (hl_* spans, HTML escaping, width-100 layout) over the clean HughesPJ engine at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/. Signature-pane parity: 82/82 byte-exact. But rule/restriction LINE WRAPPING is blocked: no single (width, ribbon) reproduces the captures. The pinned contradiction (verified by the sealed room, do trust it): in theory Scott, a nest-3 rule-BODY one-liner of content width 66 (rule c_mult) WRAPS in the captures, while a nest-3 bracket-group PREMISE of content width 66 (rule d_exp) stays one line keeping its `]` — same theory, same nest depth. A faithful HughesPJ sep/fits (budget = min(w−nest, r) − space-left) measures both identically, so no (w,r) can split them. Also: signature fsep fills demand ribbon 67 with width ≥ 78 (continuation lines reach absolute column 78 = nest 11 + 67), while rule bodies need effective width ≈ 67. And there are "pair/AC delimiter drops" — wrapped layouts in captures sometimes lose a delimiter to a space vs the clean render. Full details: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/BEHAVIOR.md sections "Web mode (R6)" and "LAYOUT BLOCKER", plus workspace/scratchpad/r6/ (panes/, fills/, raw/ pane bodies; census.py). The sealed hypotheses to check: per-element render widths; a stacked cumulative-ribbon document; a bespoke rule printer with a tighter wrap threshold; the rule-body sep (arrow at nest −1 + trailing action bracket-group) measuring 1 wider than a plain bracket-group. YOUR TASK — find the actual upstream mechanism. Read the Haskell source under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/ (pristine submodule): 1. The web pane render call chain: how `main/message` and `main/rules` pane bodies are produced — likely under src/Web/ (Theory.hs or similar) — and exactly which render function + Style (lineLength, ribbonsPerLine, mode) is applied, and WHAT UNIT is rendered per call (whole pane? per theory item? per rule?). If each item/rule is rendered by a separate render call, wrap-state does not accumulate across items — say so and give the exact per-call unit and style. 2. The rule pretty-printer used on that path (batch vs web — e.g. prettyProtoRuleACInfo vs prettyProtoRuleE or whatever it actually is): how the premise/arrow/conclusion body is assembled (sep? fsep? which nests, where the --[ ]-> arrow sits), and find the concrete measurement difference that makes c_mult wrap at content 66 while d_exp's premise group does not. Reproduce the arithmetic on those two exact lines from the captures (the sealed scratchpad r6/panes has the bodies) and CONFIRM your mechanism predicts both outcomes before you write it up. 3. The pair/AC delimiter drops: find the upstream behavior that yields a delimiter collapsing to a space in wrapped layouts (likely how punctuate/sep interacts, or a different pair printer on the web path). 4. The signature-vs-rule width tension: explain it (different render widths per section? nest offsets? the

frame?). Give the exact per-family (width, ribbon, per-call unit) table. Also read lib/utils/src/Text/PrettyPrint/Html.hs to see whether the HtmlDoc annotation wrappers change layout measurement (zero-width or not — if span open/close tags COUNT toward width anywhere, that changes fits and could be the whole story; check carefully). Cross-check any claimed mechanism against at least 3 more wrapped rule bodies from other theories in /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/scratchpad/r6/panes/ until the law predicts every one you test. If scratchpad/r6/raw is needed, the re-materialization command is in workspace/QUERIES.log. You may build/run the clean crate's diagnostic tests (cargo test in the workspace) to check predictions, but do NOT edit any sealed workspace file. Do NOT start Haskell servers; static source reading + capture arithmetic should suffice. Do not commit anything anywhere. DELIVERABLE — write ONE file: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/evidence/dirty-room/pretty_r7_layout_diagnosis.md with two clearly separated parts: PART A (open-side findings, full detail): the mechanism with upstream file/function names, the render-call table, the arithmetic on c_mult/d_exp, and your cross-check results. PART B (SEALED-SAFE INSTRUCTION BLOCK): a self-contained behavioral specification the sealed room may read. HARD RULES for part B: no Haskell identifiers, no upstream file paths, no code quotes, no expression — only behavioral laws stated as observable rendering rules ("each rule is rendered by an independent layout pass at width W ribbon R"; "the premise list is measured including/excluding X"; numbers welcome), each law paired with a capture-checkable prediction. Part B must be sufficient for a sealed implementer who has read BEHAVIOR.md to fix the wrap logic without seeing part A. End part B with a certification line that it contains no upstream identifiers or expression. Your final return message: a compact summary of the mechanism (part A level) + confirmation the file is written + whether your law survived all cross-checks or what residue remains.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the key context files and exploring the source tree structure in parallel.

content.content:
1	# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block; R2: rule blocks; R3: restriction/lemma formulas)
2	
3	Every claim traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or a pre-materialized
4	round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt` — itself oracle output). Notation:
5	"target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" = constructed
6	.spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh` (files under
7	workspace/scratchpad/probes/, outputs kept beside them as `.out`).
8	
9	## Layout engine
10	
11	HughesPJ Doc at line width 110, ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided parameters; all wrap
12	observations below are reproduced by the sanctioned BSD engine at these
13	settings). Key engine consequences confirmed against probes:
14	
15	* A one-line candidate is kept iff it fits within ribbon 73 measured from the
16	  current line position; an exactly-73-column line still fits
17	  (probe:e_mid — 73-column `equations:` one-liner kept; probe:b_all —
18	  builtins item that would end at column 75 wraps).
19	* Fill continuations align at the fill's origin column, and that indent does
20	  NOT consume ribbon: continuation content is measured from the alignment
21	  column (probe:b_all functions line 2 reaches display column 79 = 11 + 68;
22	  probe:t_wide App continuation reaches display column 78).
23	* Nested content (e.g. rule bodies at indent 3) likewise measures its ribbon
24	  from the nest, so lines up to 76 display columns appear at nest 3
25	  (probe:t_edge).
26	
27	* **Deep-structure robustness (R4 blocker 3, BYTE-NEUTRAL).** A rendered Doc is
28	  a per-LINE linked chain (`NilAbove`/`TextBeside`/`Nest` interleaved with
29	  memoised `Lazy(Forced)` thunks) as deep as the theory has lines. A huge
30	  `variants (modulo AC)` block reaches ~10 000 lines (C8's
31	  `Terminal_Receives_Records_LocalAuth_C8` is 9 886 lines; BP_IBS_2/3/4 ~5–6k),
32	  so the naive recursive `reduceDoc`/`reduceVert`/`reduceHoriz` (build), the
33	  display walk (`lay`), and — the last one to surface — the compiler-generated
34	  recursive `Drop` of the nested `Rc<Doc>` all overflow a normal 8 MB stack
35	  (measured: overflow between ~1 500 and ~2 000 two-line groups). The wide FILL
36	  machinery is already lazy and does not overflow; only these eager `Above`/
37	  spine/drop walks do. All four are rewritten to iterate over a heap `Vec`
38	  (spine unrolled then folded; `lay` a loop; an explicit-stack `Drop` for
39	  `Doc`) — a pure evaluation-order change, so every rendered byte is identical
40	  (whole existing R1/R2/R3 suites unchanged) and the four deep witness files
41	  render completely and byte-match the oracle. Proof: a 12 000-group block
42	  renders on a 2 MB stack, and the round-2 curated set (now including the four
43	  deep files) passes on a production 8 MB stack, not the former 512 MB one.
44	
45	## Signature section
46	
47	Overall shape (all targets/probes):
48	
49	```
50	// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E
51	<blank>
52	builtins: …        (line ABSENT when no surviving entries)
53	functions: …       (always present — base symbols guarantee items)
54	equations: …       (always present — base equations)
55	```
56	
57	The three declaration lines are adjacent (no blank lines between them).
58	
59	### `builtins:` line
60	
61	* Only these builtins keep a builtins-line entry: `diffie-hellman`,
62	  `bilinear-pairing`, `multiset`, `natural-numbers`, `xor`
63	  (probes:b_<name>). All others (hashing, signing, revealing-signing,
64	  symmetric-encryption, asymmetric-encryption, dest-*, locations-report)
65	  vanish from the line, contributing functions/equations instead
66	  (probes:b_*, b_locrep; target:classic_NSLPK3 has no builtins line).
67	* `bilinear-pairing` INDUCES `diffie-hellman` (probe:b_bilinear-pairing:
68	  echo `builtins: diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing`; target:sp14_Joux).
69	* Canonical order regardless of source order: diffie-hellman <
70	  bilinear-pairing < multiset < natural-numbers < xor (probe:b_all — source
71	  order was scrambled; target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
72	* Duplicates collapse (probe:b_dupline).
73	* Layout: `text "builtins: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' names)` — items
74	  comma-separated, single space, fill-wrapped, continuation aligned at
75	  column 10 (after `builtins: `), comma attached to the preceding item
76	  (probe:b_all, target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
77	
78	### `functions:` line
79	
80	* Items render `name/arity` + attribute suffix, comma-separated.
81	* Attribute suffix (probe:f_attrs, probe:b_locrep, probes:b_dest-*):
82	  - public constructor → no suffix (`c/3`)
83	  - private constructor → `[private,constructor]`
84	  - public destructor → `[destructor]`
85	  - private destructor → `[private,destructor]`
86	  No space before `[`; no spaces inside.
87	* Sort: ASCII byte order on the name, case-sensitive (`Bb/1, Zz/1, a1/2, aa/1,
88	  cA/0` — probe:f_sort).
89	* Dedup: identical (name,arity) between user decls and builtin expansions, or
90	  repeated user decls, appear once (probe:f_dedup). Conflicting arity or
91	  attribute redeclarations are REJECTED upstream by the tool
92	  (probe:f_dedup2 raw error), so the renderer never sees them.
93	* Typed declarations (`kdf(bitstring):skey`) erase to `kdf/1`
94	  (probe:f_typed).
95	* Base symbols always present: `fst/1, pair/2, snd/1` (probe:b_none);
96	  with `dest-pairing` instead: `fst/1[destructor], pair/2, snd/1[destructor]`
97	  (probe:b_dest-pairing).
98	* Per-builtin function expansions (compatibility content, from probes
99	  b_hashing / b_asymmetric-encryption / b_signing / b_symmetric-encryption /
100	  b_revealing-signing / b_locrep / b_dest-*):
101	  - hashing → `h/1`
102	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec/2, aenc/2, pk/1`
103	    (dest-asymmetric-encryption: `adec/2[destructor]`)
104	  - signing → `pk/1, sign/2, true/0, verify/3`
105	    (dest-signing: `verify/3[destructor]`)
106	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec/2, senc/2`
107	    (dest-symmetric-encryption: `sdec/2[destructor]`)
108	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage/1, pk/1, revealSign/2, revealVerify/3,
109	    true/0`
110	  - locations-report → `check_rep/2[destructor], get_rep/1[destructor],
111	    rep/2[private,constructor], report/1`
112	  - diffie-hellman / bilinear-pairing / multiset / natural-numbers / xor →
113	    none (their operators/constants — one, inv, DH_neutral, zero, ++, %+ —
114	    never appear in `functions:`; probes b_diffie-hellman, b_xor, …).
115	* Layout: `text "functions: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' items)` — fill wrap,
116	  continuation aligned at column 11 (probe:b_revealing-signing, probe:b_all,
117	  probe:e_long).
118	
119	### `equations:` block
120	
121	* Header `equations:`, or `equations [convergent]:` when the user block is
122	  declared `[convergent]` (probe:e_conv).
123	* Base equations always present: `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`,
124	  `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (probe:b_none).
125	* Per-builtin equation expansions (probes as above):
126	  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1`
127	  - signing → `verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
128	  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`
129	  - revealing-signing → `getMessage(revealSign(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`,
130	    `revealVerify(revealSign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
131	  - locations-report → `check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`,
132	    `get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`
133	  - hashing → none
134	* Sort: STRUCTURAL order on the (lhs, rhs) pair — NOT byte order of the
135	  rendered text (refuted by target:contract — `checkpcs(xc, xpk, …)` prints
136	  before `checkpcs(xc, pk(xsk), …)` though 'x' > 'p' — and by target:mesh's
137	  get_b1/get_b2/aes_cmac groups) and NOT declaration order in either
138	  direction (probes p_eqA/p_eqB and p_eqF/p_eqF2: reversed sources echo
139	  identically; contract/mesh source order is the reverse of the echo). The
140	  pinned comparison, lexicographic (lhs first, rhs breaks lhs ties —
141	  probes p_eqF/p_eqF2):
142	  - variables sort BELOW all applications regardless of names
143	    (probes p_eqC/p_eqC2: `f(zzz, …)` < `f(a0, …)` with a0/0 nullary;
144	    corpus witnesses contract `xpk` < `pk(xsk)`, mesh `cnf` <
145	    `aes_cmac(…)`);
146	  - variable vs variable: name bytes (NOT shortlex — probe:p_eqD `azz` <
147	    `b`), then index (probe:p_eqH user `x` < builtin `x.1`);
148	  - application vs application: head name bytes FIRST, then arguments
149	    left-to-right (contract checkpcs decided at argument 2; head-name byte
150	    order across groups: mesh `aes_ccm_dec` < `aes_cmac`, refuting
151	    shortlex); arity never discriminates observably except via `pair`
152	    (probe:p_eqG refutes arity-first: `pair`/2 < `z1`/1);
153	  - tuples compare as RIGHT-NESTED binary applications named `pair`
154	    (probe:p_eqE `g(…)` < `<x, y>`; probe:p_eqG `<x, y>` < `z1(…)`;
155	    probe:p_eqI `<x, zz>` < `<x, b, c>` — the binary view compares var `zz`
156	    vs app `pair(b, c)`; a flattened elementwise view would reverse them).
157	  UNOBSERVABLE ranks (cannot occur in an equations block, flagged): literal
158	  constants (rejected upstream — probe:e_samehead raw), exp/AC operators,
159	  diff, pattern-match. Implemented as application-rank under their rendered
160	  head spelling; corpus gate is the backstop if they ever occur.
161	* Dedup: EXACT duplicates collapse (probe:e_dup); alpha-equivalent but
162	  differently-named equations do NOT (probe:e_adedup keeps user
163	  `fst(<a, b>) = a` alongside the builtin fst equation).
164	* Layout: `sep (header : map (nest 4) (punctuate ',' eqDocs))` — i.e.
165	  all-or-nothing, NOT fill:
166	  - fits on one line (≤ 73) → `equations: e1, e2, e3` (probe:e_mid at
167	    exactly 73);
168	  - otherwise header alone, then EVERY equation on its own line at indent 4
169	    with trailing comma on all but the last — even when several would fit
170	    joined at indent 4 (probe:e_conv: joined length 71 still one-per-line).
171	* Each equation is `sep [lhsDoc, nest (-2) (text "= " <> rhsDoc)]`:
172	  one line `lhs = rhs`; when too long, `= rhs` drops to (equation indent − 2)
173	  (probe:e_long — `  = xlongvariablename1` at column 2 under equations at
174	  indent 4).
175	
176	## Term rendering
177	
178	Observed atoms:
179	
180	| AST shape | rendered | provenance |
181	|---|---|---|
182	| var, untagged/msg | `x` | everywhere |
183	| var, fresh | `~x` | target:cav13 (`x:fresh` source → `~x`) |
184	| var, pub | `$A` | target:cav13 |
185	| var, node | `#i` | targets (lemma text) |
186	| var, nat | `%x` | target:NumberSubtermTests |
187	| var with index k>0 | `name.k` after the sigil (`~x.7`, `x.10`, `XB.10`) | targets: variants + builtin eqs |
188	| pub literal | `'g'`, `'hello_world'` | probe:t_pair |
189	| fresh literal | `~'n'` | probe:t_frlit |
190	| nat literal 1 (`%1`, `1:nat`) | `%1` | probe:t_nat |
191	| nat literal n (`2:nat`) | `%2` | probe:t_num2 |
192	| DH one (`1` in DH theory) | `one` | probe:t_one, probe:t_gone |
193	| DH neutral | `DH_neutral` | probe:t_gone, target:cav13 variant 7 |
194	| xor zero | `zero` (nullary-app form) | probe:t_xor |
195	
196	Composite shapes:
197	
198	* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>
199	  ")"`; commas attach to the preceding arg, fill space between args, wrap
200	  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
201	  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
202	  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,
203	  `zero`, `true`, `f` — targets + probes).
204	  The application `)` is the plain BESIDE `<> ")"`, NOT a droppable fill item:
205	  when the last argument is a multi-line tuple whose `>` drops to its own line,
206	  the `)` STAYS JOINED to it as `>)` (probe:appdrop `someop(…, <…, macf(…)>)`
207	  → `…dd>)`; probe:appdrop2 nested → `…>))`; the round-2 blocker-2 corpus —
208	  ake/dh UM_three_pass etc. — has MAC applications with this exact shape). This
209	  is the crux of R4 blocker 2: the enclosing-delimiter drop is operator-
210	  specific — an AC-operator/pair `)`/`>` DROPS (fill item), an application `)`
211	  does NOT (beside). Changing `app_doc` to drop the `)` would break these
212	  witnesses; the fix is to leave it beside.
213	* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — ONE fill (`fcat`) whose items are `<`, each element
214	  with its attached `", "` under `nest 1`, and `>`:
215	  `fcat ('<' : map (nest 1) (punctuate ", " elems) ++ ['>'])`. This single
216	  construction reproduces every observed shape (probe:p_pw1 wfa–wfd,
217	  target:mesh k2 equation, probe:t_wide W1):
218	  - fits → `<a, b>` on one line; a wrapped fill line ends with the trailing
219	    space of the attached `", "` (probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked);
220	  - overflow elements continue at (column of `<`) + 1 — the nest 1;
221	  - when the FIRST element's one-liner does not fit beside `<`, the `<`
222	    stays ALONE on its line (probe:p_pw1 wfb; mesh k2 inner tuple);
223	  - `>` sits beside the last element when that element ends a fill line
224	    (p_pw1 wfa/wfb/wfd), but drops to its own line at the column of `<`
225	    (no nest) when the last element is multi-line (p_pw1 wfc; mesh k2
226	    outer tuple) — the fill places items after a multi-line item below it.
227	  The earlier R1 law (`"<" <> fcat … <> ">"`, `<`/`>` always attached) was
228	  WRONG — falsified by mesh k2 / p_pw1; it agreed with the true law only on
229	  the shapes R1 observed (fits, and wfa/wfd-style wraps).
230	  Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
231	  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
232	  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).
233	* Exponentiation: `a^b`, chains render FLAT for both left- and right-nested
234	  trees: `('g'^~x)^~y` AND `'g'^(~x^~y)` both echo `'g'^~x^~y`
235	  (probe:t_exp2). No parentheses are ever added around exp or its operands
236	  (operand classes that need delimiting — AC ops — are self-parenthesizing).
237	* AC operators mult `*`, xor `⊕` (U+2295), multiset `++`, nat-plus `%+`:
238	  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
239	  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,
240	  `('g'-exponent (~x*~y*~z))` (probes:t_xor, t_uni, t_nat, t_mult2, t_exp2 —
241	  the AC rule variants normalize (g^x)^y to `'g'^(~x*~y)`).
242	  The parens are intrinsic: they appear in every context (function argument
243	  `inv((~x.7*x.11))` — target:cav13 variants; fact argument `Out( (~x⊕~y) )`).
244	  Layout on overflow: the SAME single-fill construction as tuples, with the
245	  delimiters as fill items —
246	  `fcat ('(' : map (nest 1) (punctuate op elems) ++ [')'])`:
247	  break between elements with the operator attached to the preceding element
248	  and no fill space, continuation at (column of `(`) + 1 (probe:t_uniwide:
249	  line ends `…aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa3++`), and the `)` drops to its OWN line at
250	  the column of `(` when it does not fit beside the last element
251	  (round-3 target:alethea Universal_VerProofV_v1 — the union keeps both wide
252	  tuple elements on one 71-column fill line and only `)))"` drops). The
253	  earlier R1 law (`"(" <> fcat … <> ")"`, `)` always attached) agreed with
254	  the true construction on every R1-observed shape but was falsified by the
255	  alethea witness.
256	  The R4 blocker-2 corpus (ake/dh: UM_three_pass{,_combined,_combined_fixed},
257	  DHKEA{,_keyreg}) exercises the OTHER `)`-drop sub-case: the LAST union element
258	  is itself a multi-line tuple, so the tuple's `>` drops to its `<` column AND
259	  the union's `)` drops BELOW it to the `(` column, on SEPARATE lines
260	  (probe:uniondrop `(<'1', …>++<'2', dd, macf(…)>)` →
261	  `…>` / `…)` on their own lines; UM_three_pass echo lines ~54–55, whole-block
262	  byte-parity re-asserted). The alethea round-3 fix (`)` as a fill item)
263	  already covers this; the round-2 curated set now re-asserts it across the
264	  five ake/dh files. (Contrast the application `)` above, which stays `>)`.)
265	* diff: `diff(x, y)` — application form (probe:t_diff, run with --diff).
266	* mult inside exp exponent: `x.10^(x.11*inv(x.12))` (target:cav13) — normal
267	  AC-paren rule, no extra exp parens.
268	
269	## Rule blocks (R2)
270	
271	Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
272	blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
273	parity). A rule BLOCK is:
274	
275	```
276	rule (modulo E) Name[attrs]:
277	   <body>
278	<blank line>
279	  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
280	  <variants comment>              (col 2)
281	```
282	
283	### Header
284	
285	* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
286	  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
287	* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, process="…",
288	  no_derivcheck, issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < process <
289	  no_derivcheck < issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST
290	  color/process/role declaration wins; external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
291	  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
292	  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` single-quoted.
293	  Fill-wrap aligned after the `[`, `]:`  attached to the last item
294	  (probe:p_rattr R3, target:issue713 bla).
295	* **SAPIC `process` attribute** (R4 blocker-1 correction; probe:p_process,
296	  target:ct — the earlier "process is DROPPED" law was WRONG, from a corpus
297	  that had no `issapicrule` files in the round-2 set). It is present on every
298	  SAPIC-generated (`issapicrule`) rule, ABSENT otherwise, and renders
299	  `process="<snippet>"` — DOUBLE quotes (contrast `role='…'`), positioned
300	  between `color` and `no_derivcheck`. The `<snippet>` is the translated
301	  process step, carried VERBATIM as one unbreakable text token: `process="|"`
302	  (parallel), `process="!"` (replication), `process="0"` (null),
303	  `process="in(x.1);"`, `process="event Ev( x.1 );"`,
304	  `process="out(<x.1, 'lbl'>);"`, `process="lookup <'att', h.3> as a.1"`,
305	  `process="insert <$ca.2, 'proofOfID', $s.1, pk(skS.1)>,'yes';"`. Its interior
306	  spaces, commas, `<>`, `()`, `;` and SINGLE-quoted string constants are part
307	  of the value, NOT layout — the fill never breaks inside it (target:ct 100+
308	  witnesses; probe:p_process). ESCAPING of `"`/`\` is UNOBSERVABLE: process
309	  snippets quote their constants with `'…'`, so no `"`/`\`/control char ever
310	  reaches the attribute; rendered with no escaping (flagged — a corpus with a
311	  process containing `"`/`\` would be needed to pin it).
312	
313	### Body
314	
315	* `nest 3 (sep [prem-group, nest (-1) arrow-group, concl-group])` — one line
316	  `[ … ] --[ … ]-> [ … ]` when it fits, otherwise three rows: groups at
317	  col 3, arrow at col 2. TRUE sep, not a fill: cav13 Step1 keeps `-->` on
318	  its own row although it would fit beside the premise row.
319	* Bracket groups (premises, conclusions, AND the action arrow — one
320	  construction, probe:p_arr1): `sep [sep [open, fsep facts], close]`:
321	  - one line `[ f1, f2 ]` when the whole group fits (kept at exactly
322	    ribbon 73 — probe:p_arr1 PR);
323	  - else `close` drops to the group's column; `open` keeps ALL facts beside
324	    it iff they fit that line as a unit (target:mesh DeviceWaitingUser at
325	    exactly 73 vs ProvisionerWaitingUser one column over);
326	  - else `open` is alone too, facts fill-wrap at the group's column
327	    (target:NSLPK3 R_1 three facts per fill line; target:Tutorial Serv_1 —
328	    `--[` alone although the first action alone would fit beside it).
329	  Empty list → `[ ]`; empty actions → the literal `-->`.
330	* Fact: `sep [name<>"(" <+> fsep args, ")"]` — `Name( a, b )`, args
331	  fill-wrap aligned after `Name( `, `)` drops alone to the fact's column
332	  when the args are multi-line (probe:t_wide); `Name( )` when nullary
333	  (target:mesh); `!` prefix on persistent facts (target:NSLPK3).
334	* Fact annotations attach directly after the `)`: `[+, -, no_precomp]` —
335	  canonical order + < - < no_precomp regardless of source order
336	  (probe:p_fann `[no_precomp,+]` → `[+, no_precomp]`;
337	  target:seqdfsneeded `[no_precomp,-]` → `[-, no_precomp]`).
338	
339	### Loop breakers
340	
341	* `// loop breaker: [0]` singular / `// loop breakers: [0,1]` plural,
342	  0-based premise indices, comma WITHOUT space (probes c_loop, p_lb2).
343	* E-rule side: on its own line at col 2, between the blank line and the
344	  variants comment. AC-rule side: INSIDE the comment at col 4, AFTER the
345	  variants list (probe:p_lbvar).
346	
347	### The variants comment
348	
349	* Trivial: `/* has exactly the trivial AC variant */` at col 2.
350	* Otherwise (col 2 `/*` … `*/`):
351	  ```
352	  /*
353	  rule (modulo AC) Name:
354	     <body, same layout, nested +2>
355	    variants (modulo AC)          (col 4; ABSENT when no substitutions —
356	    <numbered groups>              target:issue777 macro-expanded AC rule)
357	    // loop breaker: [..]         (optional, col 4)
358	  */
359	  ```
360	* Numbered groups at col 4: the index is RIGHT-ALIGNED to the widest
361	  `N.` (`1.` cav13/7 variants; ` 9.`/`12.` CH07; `  1.`/`160.` Joux),
362	  followed by one space; the group's substitution lines align after that
363	  prefix. Groups are separated by a line of BARE INDENT (four spaces at
364	  this nesting — trailing whitespace, byte-checked probe:p_var1); no
365	  separator after the last group.
366	* One substitution = `lhs $$ nest 6 ("= " <> rhs)` with HughesPJ overlap:
367	  a lhs narrower than 6 columns is padded to column 6 (`~lv2  = ~lv2.4`,
368	  `~ltkS = …` at exactly 5+1); a lhs of ≥ 6 columns pushes `= rhs` to its
369	  own line at column 6 relative to the entry (probe:p_var1
370	  `~longvariablenameone`, target:Tutorial `request`). The rhs is a full R1
371	  term with its own wrapping (target:mesh multi-line aes_cmac values).
372	  The lhs/rhs substitution DATA is solver-provided input; both sides render
373	  through the R1 term core.
374	
375	### `macros:` block (rule-adjacent surface)
376	
377	* `text "macros: " <> vcat (punctuate ',' items)` — the block ALWAYS breaks
378	  (R5 GAP-2 correction; the earlier `sep`/all-or-nothing law was WRONG). The
379	  first macro sits beside `macros: ` and every subsequent macro goes on its
380	  own line aligned after `macros: ` (col 8), REGARDLESS of fit
381	  (probe:r5_mac2 — two short macros that would fit one line still break;
382	  probe:p_mac1; target:issue777 / probe:r5_mac1 — a lone macro is trivially
383	  one line). Commas attach to the preceding macro; the last carries none.
384	  (`sep` agreed with the true `vcat` law only when the list overflowed, which
385	  every earlier witness happened to do — probe:r5_mac2 is the first
386	  fits-but-still-breaks witness.)
387	* Item: fact-style head with the `)` ATTACHED to the last param line
388	  (unlike facts), then `) =  body` — two spaces after `=` (the `= ` token
389	  plus hsep spacing); the body always sits beside and wraps internally
390	  (probe:p_mac1 wide macro).
391	
392	### Out of R2 scope (recorded, not implemented here)
393	
394	* The theory-level `/* looping facts with injective instances: A/1 */`
395	  comment (probe:c_loop) sits between the signature and the rules — top
396	  level assembly, not the rule block.
397	* Embedded rule restrictions (`_restrict`) never surfaced in any probed
398	  echo (TPM_DAA corpus files lift them to top-level restrictions upstream);
399	  no rendering law claimed.
400	* Diff-mode (`--diff`) rule surfaces (left/right variants) are not covered
401	  by the round-2 curated set; a later round must pin them before diff-file
402	  parity is claimed.
403	
404	## Formula rendering (R3)
405	
406	Curated byte targets: round3/targets/*.hs.txt (20 corpus files; 84
407	restriction + 139 lemma blocks, every block byte-verified by
408	tests/round3_formulas.rs round-trip parity).
409	
410	### Glyphs and atoms
411	
412	| construct | rendered | provenance |
413	|---|---|---|
414	| true / false | `⊤` / `⊥` | probe:q_w1 |
415	| conjunction / disjunction | `∧` / `∨` | probe:q_at1, q_p2 |
416	| implication / iff | `⇒` / `⇔` | probe:q_p2, q_r2 |
417	| negation | `¬(…)` — argument ALWAYS parenthesized | probe:q_p2 s8 |
418	| quantifiers | `∀` / `∃` | everywhere |
419	| action atom | `Fact( … ) @ tp` — spaces around `@` | probe:q_at1 |
420	| temporal/nat order | `t1 < t2` | probe:q_at1 |
421	| equality | `t1 = t2` | probe:q_at1 |
422	| subterm | `t1 ⊏ t2` (source `<<` and `⊏` both echo `⊏`) | probe:q_at1, target:NumberSubtermTests |
423	| last | `last(tp)` — NO interior spaces (unlike facts) | probe:q_at1 |
424	
425	Terms inside atoms render through the R1 term core unchanged (probe:q_l4 —
426	pair trailing-`", "` wrap, AC self-parens); action facts through the R2 fact
427	construction unchanged, `!` prefix included (target:NSLPK3 `!KU( ni ) @ #j`,
428	probe:q_l3).
429	
430	### Parenthesization
431	
432	* EVERY operand of a binary connective (∧ ∨ ⇒ ⇔) is wrapped in `(…)`,
433	  whatever it is — atom, ⊤/⊥, ¬, quantifier, another connective
434	  (probe:q_p2 s1–s13; targets NSLPK3/Cronto/acc).
435	* `¬`'s argument is always wrapped (double wrap when ¬ is itself an
436	  operand: `(¬(x = 'd'))`).
437	* Quantifier BODIES and the top level are bare (probe:q_p2, q_w1).
438	* Chains render their source association faithfully — `a | b | c` parses
439	  left-nested upstream and echoes `((a) ∨ (b)) ∨ (c)`; no flattening,
440	  no re-association (probe:q_at1, probe:q_p2 s3–s6).
441	
442	### Layout
443	
444	* Binary connective = `sep [lhs-operand <+> glyph, rhs-operand]`: one line
445	  `(A) ∧ (B)` when it fits; otherwise the glyph stays on the lhs' LAST line
446	  and the rhs drops to the group origin (targets NSLPK3 types /
447	  Yubikey slightly_weaker_invariant / Cronto notSameRole deep left chain).
448	* Quantifier = `sep [glyph<>" "<>fsep binders<>".", nest 1 body]`:
449	  - binders are space-separated in source order with their R1 sigils and
450	    `.idx` suffixes (`∀ x.1 a.1 #i.1.` — probe:q_b1, target:acc lemmas);
451	  - the binder fill wraps aligned after `"∀ "` (origin+2 — probe:q_l2 bw1);
452	  - the `.` attaches to the last binder;
453	  - the body sits beside after one space, or drops to (quantifier origin+1)
454	    — the nest 1 (probe:q_l2 bw1/bw2; paren-nested `(((∃ #j.` → body at
455	    paren col + 2 confirms the same nest at depth).
456	* Relation atoms (`=` `<` `⊏`) = `sep [lhs-term <+> glyph, rhs-term]`: glyph
457	  attached to the lhs line, rhs drops to the atom origin (probe:q_l4 tw1–3).
458	* Action atom = `hsep [fact, "@", timepoint]`: `@ tp` NEVER drops alone —
459	  at overflow the FACT breaks internally (its `)` drops to the fact column,
460	  `@ tp` beside it: `) @ #i` — probe:q_l5 m63/m64 pins hsep over the
461	  sep-alternative, which would have kept `fact ) @` on one line).
462	* One-line fits follow the engine at 110/73 with the ribbon measured from
463	  the nest; the closing quote/parens attached after a formula count against
464	  its last line's fit (probe:q_l5 — 73-content + `"` broke).
465	
466	## Restriction blocks (R3)
467	
468	```
469	restriction Name:
470	  "formula"                (nest 2; quotes attach directly around the doc)
471	  // safety formula        (col 2 — iff the formula classifies safety)
472	<blank>
473	  /*                       (comment at nest 2)
474	  expanded formula:
475	  "formula"
476	  */
477	```
478	
479	* The `axiom` keyword echoes as `restriction` with the identical wrapper
480	  (probe:q_ax1, target:Cronto_EA).
481	* **Statement vs expanded formula (R5 GAP-1).** The STATEMENT renders in MACRO
482	  form (as written); the `expanded formula:` comment renders in the
483	  macro/predicate-EXPANDED form. They are TWO DISTINCT formula values whenever
484	  the restriction uses a macro (target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions — statement
485	  `A( m(m3(x)) )`, expanded `A( x )`). PREDICATE expansion happens upstream of
486	  BOTH renderings, so a predicate-only restriction still shows the two
487	  identically (probe:q_pred1, target:features_predicates_minimal), and every
488	  macro-free restriction renders the same formula twice (the earlier "byte-
489	  identical in every observation" law — a special case, since no earlier probe
490	  had a macro). The expanded formula is a caller-supplied opaque input (the
491	  ported macro expansion), modeled as `Restriction.expanded`; safety is
492	  classified on the statement (macro expansion is term-level, so quantifier
493	  structure — hence the safety verdict — is identical either way).
494	* LEMMAS with macros have NO separate expanded-formula comment: the STATEMENT
495	  is the macro form (`l.formula`) and the guarded comment carries the EXPANDED
496	  form as opaque input (already handled by `Guarded`) — confirmed for
497	  exists-trace (target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions) and all-traces
498	  (probe:r5_allmacro: statement `Ev( g(x) )`, guarded `Ev( h(x) )`). No lemma
499	  model change was needed.
500	* Safety classification (pinned by probes q_s1/q_s2 + q_w1 + 84-block corpus
501	  parity): a formula is safety iff its negation-normal form contains NO
502	  existential quantifier, msg-sort and temporal alike. Every ⇒-antecedent
503	  flips polarity; `¬∃` in a conclusion (∀ in NNF) keeps safety, `¬∃` in an
504	  antecedent (∃ in NNF) defeats it; a non-temporal `∃ y.` conclusion defeats
505	  it; top-level `¬(∃ …)` restrictions are safety (target:Yubikey).
506	* Sort-order of the comment lines: statement, safety line, blank, comment —
507	  the safety line sits BETWEEN the formula and the blank (probe:q_w1 r_eq).
508	
509	## Lemma blocks (R3)
510	
511	```
512	lemma Name [attr1, attr2]:
513	  all-traces|exists-trace "formula"    (sep at nest 2 — one line iff it fits)
514	/*                                     (comment at col 0)
515	guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:      (| all satisfying traces:)
516	"<guarded block>"                      (opaque input, verbatim lines)
517	*/
518	by sorry                               (| the embedded proof, verbatim)
519	```
520	
521	* Header: `lemma Name:`; with attributes `lemma Name [` + fill + `]:` — a
522	  SPACE before `[` (unlike rule attributes), items comma+space separated,
523	  fill-wrapped aligned after the `[` with `]:` attached to the last item;
524	  the first item stays beside the `[` even past the width (target:5G_AKA
525	  weakagreement_… at 120 display columns).
526	* Attributes render in SOURCE order, duplicates kept — NO canonicalization
527	  (probe:q_la1 `[hide_lemma=la1, use_induction, hide_lemma=la3, reuse]`).
528	  Spellings: `sources`, `reuse`, `use_induction`, `hide_lemma=<name>`,
529	  `heuristic=<value>` with the goal-ranking value verbatim (`S`, `{mytac}` —
530	  probes q_la2/q_la3; corpus `{sqn}` etc.).
531	* The trace-quantifier keyword is ALWAYS on the statement line, never the
532	  header line, even for `"⊤"` (probe:q_w1 l_top).
533	* Guarded comment at col 0, directly after the statement (no blank line):
534	  header `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:` for
535	  all-traces, `… all satisfying traces:` for exists-trace (probe:q_w1). The
536	  quoted content is OPAQUE pre-computed input (the ported guarded transform's
537	  rendering — note its style differs from the statement printer: n-ary ∧
538	  rows, lone `∧`/`⇒` lines, parenthesized ∃-body atoms; never produced by
539	  this crate).
540	* Failed conversion variant (probe:q_r1): header
541	  `conversion to guarded formula failed:` followed by the transform's error
542	  text with every line indented +2 (the raw error spelling, observed
543	  unindented in the fatal-restriction case, gains exactly 2 columns here).
544	* Tail: `by sorry` at col 0 when the source had no proof; a source-embedded
545	  proof re-renders after the `*/` instead (target:Yubikey `induction … qed`)
546	  — that proof text is PORTED-renderer output, carried verbatim as input.
547	* Restrictions outside the guarded fragment are a FATAL load error
548	  (probe:q_p1 raw), so the restriction renderer never sees them; lemmas
549	  outside it load fine and take the failed-conversion comment (probe:q_r1).
550	
551	## Theory frame (R5 GAP-3)
552	
553	Whole-echo assembly `theory <name> begin … end`. Curated byte targets: the
554	round1-3 `targets/*.hs.txt` whole captures; 29 diverse files
555	(tests/round5_theory.rs `whole_echo_frame_parity`) byte-verified end-to-end
556	(builtins variety, macros, rules ± variants, loop breakers, SAPIC process
557	attrs, restrictions ± safety ± expanded, lemmas all/exists ± attrs ± embedded
558	proof, predicates, heuristic, tactic, section comments).
559	
560	* **Header / footer.** Always `theory NAME` then a blank, `begin`, a blank
561	  (all 69 surveyed captures). No `configuration` string observed. Close: the
562	  extracted echo ALWAYS ends with exactly THREE blank lines then `end` (all 44
563	  surveyed captures). Those blanks are the residue the gate extraction leaves
564	  after dropping the two trailing comment blocks that are OUT of this crate's
565	  span — the wellformedness report and the `Generated from:` stamp — each a
566	  single blank-separated slot, plus the blank before `end` (RAW tail of
567	  target:MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions verified: `by sorry` · blank · wf-line ·
568	  blank · Generated-block · blank · `end`).
569	* **Signature first.** The signature block (`// Function signature …` + blank +
570	  `builtins:`/`functions:`/`equations:`) is ALWAYS the first thing after
571	  `begin`, even ahead of `tactic:` blocks (target:5G_AKA) and `heuristic:`
572	  (target:contract). It is rendered from the `Signature`, never a theory item.
573	* **Item order + spacing.** All other blocks are theory items in SOURCE order;
574	  the frame stacks them with ONE blank line between successive items. Item
575	  types observed: `macros:`, `predicate:` (grouped), rules, restrictions,
576	  lemmas, `heuristic:`, `tactic:`, `section{* … *}` formal comments, and the
577	  theory-level `/* looping facts with injective instances: … */` note.
578	  Assembly = `join(["theory NAME", "begin", sig, items…], "\n\n") + "\n\n\n\nend"`.
579	* **`heuristic:` line.** A single `heuristic: <value>` item (targets contract,
580	  ct, running-example, accountability — value e.g. `p`, `o "oracle" …`).
581	* **`predicate:` block.** One `predicate: <fact><=><formula>` per predicate; a
582	  contiguous run renders as one item, blank-line separated. NO spaces around
583	  `<=>`; the head is the R2 fact (`True( x )`), the body the R3 formula. The
584	  body wraps at ABSOLUTE MARGIN 0 (column-1 nest), INDEPENDENT of the header
585	  width (target:dmn-basic — `Sender_duplicate` and `Mixer_duplicate` bodies
586	  both wrap at col 1 despite different name lengths, and a 66-column body row
587	  fits ribbon 73 measured from col 1, not from the `<=>` column). A
588	  `<>`-beside composition would instead indent the body under `<=>` — the
589	  sanctioned HughesPJ `display` threads the current line width into each
590	  `NilAbove` continuation (`lay2 k (NilAbove p) = nl `txt` lay k p`;
591	  pretty-1.1.3.6 confirmed) — so the renderer splices the header textually onto
592	  the margin-0 formula's first line instead of `<>`-ing it.
593	* **Opaque / verbatim items.** `tactic:` (the whole contiguous region — sub-
594	  tactics `presort:`/`prio:` carry NO blank-line separators, so the region is
595	  one block; target:5G_AKA/alethea), `section{* … *}` formal comments
596	  (target:Joux/Yubikey/contract), and top-level `/* … */` comments (looping-
597	  facts note) are carried through the frame VERBATIM — their interior layout is
598	  outside this crate's erasure surface, like guarded/proof text.
599	
600	## UNOBSERVABLE (recorded per protocol, not guessed as pinned behavior)
601	
602	* PatMatch (`=x` pattern-match marker, sapic): cannot be forced through the
603	  MSR no-prove echo. Code renders `=` + term as a placeholder, flagged to
604	  this entry; must be pinned before any sapic-surface parity is claimed.
605	* SortHint::Suffix(s): the echo only ever shows sigil form (source `x:fresh`
606	  echoes `~x`, target:cav13), so suffix-tagged vars are rendered with the
607	  equivalent sigil.
608	* exp/mult element-level break choice: wide mult probes exceed the
609	  variant-computation budget (probe:t_mult timed out; 4-way
610	  probe:t_xorwide too). Union (probe:t_uniwide, round-3 target:alethea `)`
611	  drop) and Xor (probe:t_xorwide3, 3-way with long names: break after `⊕`
612	  attached to the preceding element, continuation after the `(`) pin the
613	  fill construction directly; Mult/NatPlus/exp-chains use the same
614	  construction by structural analogy — flagged, to be confirmed by the
615	  full-corpus gate at integration.
616	* reliable-channel builtin: requires a sapic top-level process (tool refuses
617	  otherwise — probe:b_reliable-channel raw); its signature contribution is
618	  unpinned in R1.
619	* Adapter contract notes: `NatLit`/`Number` carry DIGITS (renderer prefixes
620	  `%`); `NumberOne` is the DH `one`; `NatOne` is `%1`.
621	* Atom::LessMset (multiset smaller): no corpus witness and no reachable
622	  source spelling found (`(<)` absent from the whole corpus); rendered like
623	  `Less` as a flagged placeholder — must be pinned before any claim.
624	* Atom::Pred (predicate fact atoms): predicates are expanded upstream of the
625	  echo in BOTH the restriction statement and the expanded-formula comment
626	  (probe:q_pred1) and in sapic lemmas (corpus timepoints.spthy), so a Pred
627	  atom never reaches the no-prove renderer; rendered as a bare fact,
628	  flagged.
629	* Diff-mode surfaces (`--diff`): diffLemma items, lemma/restriction
630	  `left`/`right` attributes, `diff_reuse`. The parity corpus EXCLUDES all
631	  --diff files (RS cannot load them), so these never reach the gate; not
632	  modeled in the crate AST. A later round must pin them before any
633	  diff-file parity is claimed.
634	* Lemma modulo annotation (`lemma (modulo E) …`): never appears in any
635	  round-3 capture (grep across all 20); not modeled.
636	* Lemma attributes beyond the observed five (`output=…`, `Hint`, prover
637	  internals): no corpus/probe witness under the no-prove echo; not modeled.
638	* Fact annotations (`[+]`/`[-]`/`[no_precomp]`) on ACTION atoms inside
639	  formulas: never observed in any formula echo; the test parser rejects
640	  them loudly if they ever appear.
641	* ⇔ inside a safety-relevant restriction: no witness (⇔ restrictions are
642	  rare-to-absent in the corpus); the classifier treats each ⇔ side as
643	  occurring in both polarities (the NNF-consistent reading), flagged.
644	* Guarded-formula content and embedded proof scripts are PORTED-renderer
645	  output consumed verbatim as input — their interior layout is not a claim
646	  of this crate (only the comment frame and headers are).
647	* Restrictions whose formula falls outside the guarded fragment: fatal
648	  upstream error (probe:q_p1), unreachable for the renderer.
649	
650	## Web mode (R6) — the interactive server's HTML rendering of theory content
651	
652	The interactive server renders the SAME theory blocks the batch echo models, but
653	as HTML fragment bodies for the `main/message` and `main/rules` panes (and the
654	source views). Derived entirely from the 82 captured crawl manifests
655	(weblayer/producers/oracle/captured_responses; sliced by extract_fragments.py
656	into scratchpad/r6/panes) and doc-engine probes — no tamarin HS source read. The
657	producers-clean skin (escape_text / postprocess_lines / `{html,title}` envelope,
658	weblayer BEHAVIOR §2–4) consumes these bodies as OPAQUE input and wraps them;
659	R6 PRODUCES the bodies. Acceptance composes R6 output through that skin and
660	byte-compares the whole captured pane body.
661	
662	### Layout parameters — width 100, ribbon 67 (NOT the batch 110/73)
663	
664	DEFINITIVE (scratchpad zz_width_probe, deleted): all 139 signature
665	`functions:`/`builtins:` fills reconstructed from the corpus render byte-identical
666	at `render_with(100, 67, doc)`; ribbon 66 misses 10, ribbon 68 misses 9. These
667	are the default HughesPJ `Style` (`lineLength = 100`, `ribbonsPerLine = 1.5`,
668	`round(100/1.5) = 67`); batch mode is the same 1.5 ratio at lineLength 110
669	(`round(110/1.5) = 73`). A fill item is kept on a line iff its END column ≤ the
670	ribbon (probe:ribbon_boundary), but the fill has HughesPJ lookahead so this is
671	NOT a per-item greedy rule — only the engine reproduces it.
672	
673	### The escape set — identical to producers `escape_text`
674	
675	`& < > " '` → `& < > " '` (verified: the corpus entity
676	inventory of message/rules bodies is exactly `' > < "` plus
677	`&`; no `'`, no `\uXXXX`). Backslash and non-ASCII pass raw (the formula
678	glyphs `∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ ⊥ ⊏ ⊤ ⊐` are raw UTF-8). Leading indent spaces are NOT
679	converted here — the producers postprocess turns them into ` ` runs. So an
680	R6 body line carries: escaped content + `hl_*` spans + RAW leading spaces, one
681	logical line per `\n`.
682	
683	### The `hl_*` span vocabulary (census over all 82+82 bodies)
684	
685	`hl_keyword` — 8 token kinds, the span covers the KEYWORD ONLY (incl the trailing
686	colon of a declaration keyword, but NOT any trailing space):
687	  * `rule`, `modulo` (every rule header AND every AC re-render inside a variants
688	    comment; two separate spans, the `(`/`)`/name between them PLAIN);
689	  * `variants` (the `variants (modulo AC)` line — `variants` spanned, the rest
690	    plain);
691	  * `restriction` (restriction header; name+`:` plain);
692	  * `functions:`, `equations:`, `builtins:`, `equations [convergent]:`
693	    (declaration keywords, colon INCLUDED).
694	  Rule NAME, attribute list and the attribute brackets `[color=…, process="…"]`
695	  are PLAIN (never spanned).
696	
697	`hl_operator` — 18 token kinds:
698	  * rule structure `[`  `]`  `--[`  `]->`(`]->`)  `-->`(`-->`);
699	  * formula connective/negation parens `(` `)` — ONLY the connective-operand and
700	    `¬`-argument parens; application `f(…)`, fact `Name( … )`, AC-operator
701	    `(a*b)` and pair `<…>` delimiters are NEVER spanned (pair/AC `<`/`>` are only
702	    entity-escaped);
703	  * `=` (equation `=` AND the `Eq` atom `=`), `<` (the `Less`/temporal atom,
704	    escaped `<`), `@` (action-at), `.` (quantifier dot after binders);
705	  * `∀ ` / `∃ ` (glyph + trailing space, one span), `⇒`, `∧`, `∨`, `¬`, `⊥`.
706	  Observed-absent, rendered by pattern and FLAGGED (no corpus witness): `⊤`
707	  (true), `⊏` (subterm), `⇔` (iff), `last(…)`, `⊐`. A variants-substitution `= `
708	  (`~lv2  = ~lv2.4`) is PLAIN, not `hl_operator` (distinct from the equation `=`).
709	  Binders, timepoints and all terms/facts inside atoms are PLAIN.
710	
711	`hl_comment` — the whole comment is ONE span, tags need not balance per line:
712	  * `/* has exactly the trivial AC variant */` (single line);
713	  * the multi-line `/* … variants (modulo AC) … */` block: span OPENS at `/*`,
714	    CLOSES after `*/`, and the AC-rule re-render INSIDE it keeps its own nested
715	    `hl_keyword`/`hl_operator` spans (spans NEST);
716	  * the restriction `/* expanded formula: "…" */` block: same nesting (the
717	    `expanded formula:` text plain, the quoted formula's operator spans nested);
718	  * `// safety formula`, `// loop breaker: [n]`, `// loop breakers: [i,j]`.
719	  A comment's leading indent spaces are OUTSIDE the span (raw, before the open).
720	
721	### Content structure vs the batch echo
722	
723	* NO signature header comment: the `Signature`/message body starts directly at
724	  `builtins:` / `functions:` (the batch `// Function signature …` line is absent).
725	* `main/message` bodies (three Keep-mode sections, producers §5): `Signature` =
726	  the builtins/functions/equations block at web params, no header; `Construction
727	  Rules` / `Deconstruction Rules` = the intruder rules rendered BARE — header +
728	  body ONLY, NO variants comment / loop breaker — joined by ONE blank line.
729	* `main/rules` bodies (producers §6): `Fact Symbols …` = one OPAQUE input line
730	  (`None`, or `L_x(id,?,?), …` with `?`/`=`/`.`/`≤`/`<` placeholder chars,
731	  entity-escaped, NO spans — carried through, not modeled); `Multiset Rewriting
732	  Rules` = FULL rule blocks (header + body + blank + `/* variant */`), joined by
733	  TWO blank lines; `Restrictions of the Set of Traces` = restriction blocks
734	  (statement, `// safety formula` when safety, blank, `/* expanded formula: */`),
735	  joined by one blank; `Macros` slot when present (UNOBSERVED in the 82-manifest
736	  corpus — needs a live macros theory).
737	* The restriction wrapper quotes (`"…"`) are PLAIN; the formula's
738	  operator spans sit inside. Statement and expanded-formula render the two
739	  distinct formula values (batch R5 GAP-1) at web params.
740	
741	### Implementation (pretty-clean, `web.rs` + span-site edits)
742	
743	One model, two render targets: the SAME block-doc builders
744	(signature/rule/formula/lemma) emit glyphs through `hl_*` wrappers that, in web
745	mode, wrap the glyph in a zero-width span-marker pair (mirroring the sanctioned
746	`Annotated.HughesPJ` `AnnotStart`/`AnnotEnd`: `sized_text(0, …)` sentinels that
747	flow through best/fits/lay at zero width, so LAYOUT is identical to plain), and
748	in batch mode are the identity (byte-identical to R1–R5). `render_html`
749	= `render_with(100, 67, doc)` then a post-pass that entity-escapes and expands
750	the sentinels into `<span class="hl_…">`/`</span>`. Multi-line comment spans fall
751	out naturally (open marker before `/*`, close after `*/`, `\n`s between). No
752	change to the doc engine.
753	
754	### Web mode (R6) — LAYOUT BLOCKER (line wrapping of sep-based constructs)
755	
756	STATUS: span vocabulary, entity escaping, section structure/separators and token
757	CONTENT are reproduced and validated corpus-wide; the SIGNATURE bodies render
758	byte-identical (all 82 message-pane signatures — tests/round6_web.rs
759	`signature_pane_sweep`, `signature_mutation_check`). The residual is line-WRAPPING
760	of the `sep`-based constructs (rule bodies, restriction quantifiers) and the
761	pair/AC delimiter drops.
762	
763	PROVEN IMPOSSIBILITY at any single (width, ribbon) with the faithful HughesPJ
764	engine (the sanctioned pretty-1.1.3.6, which the batch R1–R5 output validates):
765	* A nest-3 rule-BODY one-liner of content 66 (`c_mult`:
766	  `[ !KU( x ), !KU( x.1 ) ] --[ !KU( (x*x.1) ) ]-> [ !KU( (x*x.1) ) ]`) WRAPS to
767	  three rows in the captures, while a nest-3 bracket-group PREMISE of content 66
768	  (`d_exp`: `[ !KD( x.5^(x.4*x.6*inv((x.2*x.7))) ), !KU( (x.2*x.3*inv(x.4)) ) ]`)
769	  KEEPS its `]` — both in the SAME theory (Scott), same nest. This engine (and
770	  the sanctioned `sep1`/`sepNB`/`fits`, budget `min(w-nest, r) - sl`) measures
771	  the two IDENTICALLY (both content 66), so they wrap together at every (w,r)
772	  (verified: both flip at width 69 / ribbon 67). Tamarin distinguishes them.
773	* Independently: the signature `fsep` continuations reach absolute 78 (= nest 11
774	  + ribbon 67), which needs ribbon 67 AND width ≥ 78; the nest-3 rule bodies wrap
775	  at content 66, which needs an effective width ~67. No single width satisfies
776	  both.
777	Diagnostic sweep (tests/round6_web.rs `sweep_count`, IGNORED) at (100, 67):
778	byte-identical 206/367 sections; token-CONTENT (spans stripped, unescaped,
779	whitespace-collapsed) 331/367; SPAN-placement (class + normalized inner) 348/367.
780	Every content/span miss inspected is layout-induced — a dropped pair/app
781	delimiter (`…>) )` vs `…>)`) collapsing to a stray space, or a comment/inner
782	span whose wrapped text differs; NOT a token or escaping bug. (Exception: 1 sig
783	content miss is a harness artifact — `parse_signature` cannot recover
784	`dest-pairing` from the shown functions, so it re-adds a constructor `fst/1`; the
785	real adapter carries the builtin set, so the byte sweep uses a `dest-pairing`
786	heuristic and passes 82/82.)
787	
788	WORKING HYPOTHESES for a successor / the dirty room (pick one to confirm):
789	* Tamarin's web does NOT single-render each pane with `renderStyle defaultStyle`;
790	  likely a per-element render (signature vs rules at different effective widths),
791	  OR the pane is one stacked document where the HughesPJ ribbon mechanism
792	  (`get (w - sl)` after each `NilAbove`) narrows deeper content cumulatively, OR
793	  a bespoke rule/formula printer wraps bodies on a tighter threshold than `sep`.
794	* The 1-column c_mult-vs-d_exp discrepancy hints the rule-BODY `sep`
795	  (which carries the arrow's `nest (-1)` and a nested action bracket-group) is
796	  measured 1 wider than a plain bracket-group in tamarin — worth an isolated
797	  dirty-room probe of `prettyProtoRuleACInfo`/the web `render` call and its width.
798	Pinned params in `web.rs` are (100, 67) — correct for the signature fills and
799	the canonical default style; the rule/restriction wrap thresholds await this
800	resolution. The span-injection / escaping / structure code is complete and
801	correct and does not depend on the width fix.
802

file.content:
# BEHAVIOR.md — inferred behavioral spec of the theory echo (R1: term core + signature block; R2: rule blocks; R3: restriction/lemma formulas)

Every claim traces to a logged oracle probe (QUERIES.log) or a pre-materialized
round-1 capture (`round1/targets/*.hs.txt` — itself oracle output). Notation:
"target:<file>" = observed in that capture; "probe:<name>" = constructed
.spthy run through `oracle/pretty_oracle.sh` (files under
workspace/scratchpad/probes/, outputs kept beside them as `.out`).

## Layout engine

HughesPJ Doc at line width 110, ribbon 73 (SPEC-provided parameters; all wrap
observations below are reproduced by the sanctioned BSD engine at these
settings). Key engine consequences confirmed against probes:

* A one-line candidate is kept iff it fits within ribbon 73 measured from the
  current line position; an exactly-73-column line still fits
  (probe:e_mid — 73-column `equations:` one-liner kept; probe:b_all —
  builtins item that would end at column 75 wraps).
* Fill continuations align at the fill's origin column, and that indent does
  NOT consume ribbon: continuation content is measured from the alignment
  column (probe:b_all functions line 2 reaches display column 79 = 11 + 68;
  probe:t_wide App continuation reaches display column 78).
* Nested content (e.g. rule bodies at indent 3) likewise measures its ribbon
  from the nest, so lines up to 76 display columns appear at nest 3
  (probe:t_edge).

* **Deep-structure robustness (R4 blocker 3, BYTE-NEUTRAL).** A rendered Doc is
  a per-LINE linked chain (`NilAbove`/`TextBeside`/`Nest` interleaved with
  memoised `Lazy(Forced)` thunks) as deep as the theory has lines. A huge
  `variants (modulo AC)` block reaches ~10 000 lines (C8's
  `Terminal_Receives_Records_LocalAuth_C8` is 9 886 lines; BP_IBS_2/3/4 ~5–6k),
  so the naive recursive `reduceDoc`/`reduceVert`/`reduceHoriz` (build), the
  display walk (`lay`), and — the last one to surface — the compiler-generated
  recursive `Drop` of the nested `Rc<Doc>` all overflow a normal 8 MB stack
  (measured: overflow between ~1 500 and ~2 000 two-line groups). The wide FILL
  machinery is already lazy and does not overflow; only these eager `Above`/
  spine/drop walks do. All four are rewritten to iterate over a heap `Vec`
  (spine unrolled then folded; `lay` a loop; an explicit-stack `Drop` for
  `Doc`) — a pure evaluation-order change, so every rendered byte is identical
  (whole existing R1/R2/R3 suites unchanged) and the four deep witness files
  render completely and byte-match the oracle. Proof: a 12 000-group block
  renders on a 2 MB stack, and the round-2 curated set (now including the four
  deep files) passes on a production 8 MB stack, not the former 512 MB one.

## Signature section

Overall shape (all targets/probes):

```
// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E
<blank>
builtins: …        (line ABSENT when no surviving entries)
functions: …       (always present — base symbols guarantee items)
equations: …       (always present — base equations)
```

The three declaration lines are adjacent (no blank lines between them).

### `builtins:` line

* Only these builtins keep a builtins-line entry: `diffie-hellman`,
  `bilinear-pairing`, `multiset`, `natural-numbers`, `xor`
  (probes:b_<name>). All others (hashing, signing, revealing-signing,
  symmetric-encryption, asymmetric-encryption, dest-*, locations-report)
  vanish from the line, contributing functions/equations instead
  (probes:b_*, b_locrep; target:classic_NSLPK3 has no builtins line).
* `bilinear-pairing` INDUCES `diffie-hellman` (probe:b_bilinear-pairing:
  echo `builtins: diffie-hellman, bilinear-pairing`; target:sp14_Joux).
* Canonical order regardless of source order: diffie-hellman <
  bilinear-pairing < multiset < natural-numbers < xor (probe:b_all — source
  order was scrambled; target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).
* Duplicates collapse (probe:b_dupline).
* Layout: `text "builtins: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' names)` — items
  comma-separated, single space, fill-wrapped, continuation aligned at
  column 10 (after `builtins: `), comma attached to the preceding item
  (probe:b_all, target:features_multiset_NumberSubtermTests).

### `functions:` line

* Items render `name/arity` + attribute suffix, comma-separated.
* Attribute suffix (probe:f_attrs, probe:b_locrep, probes:b_dest-*):
  - public constructor → no suffix (`c/3`)
  - private constructor → `[private,constructor]`
  - public destructor → `[destructor]`
  - private destructor → `[private,destructor]`
  No space before `[`; no spaces inside.
* Sort: ASCII byte order on the name, case-sensitive (`Bb/1, Zz/1, a1/2, aa/1,
  cA/0` — probe:f_sort).
* Dedup: identical (name,arity) between user decls and builtin expansions, or
  repeated user decls, appear once (probe:f_dedup). Conflicting arity or
  attribute redeclarations are REJECTED upstream by the tool
  (probe:f_dedup2 raw error), so the renderer never sees them.
* Typed declarations (`kdf(bitstring):skey`) erase to `kdf/1`
  (probe:f_typed).
* Base symbols always present: `fst/1, pair/2, snd/1` (probe:b_none);
  with `dest-pairing` instead: `fst/1[destructor], pair/2, snd/1[destructor]`
  (probe:b_dest-pairing).
* Per-builtin function expansions (compatibility content, from probes
  b_hashing / b_asymmetric-encryption / b_signing / b_symmetric-encryption /
  b_revealing-signing / b_locrep / b_dest-*):
  - hashing → `h/1`
  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec/2, aenc/2, pk/1`
    (dest-asymmetric-encryption: `adec/2[destructor]`)
  - signing → `pk/1, sign/2, true/0, verify/3`
    (dest-signing: `verify/3[destructor]`)
  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec/2, senc/2`
    (dest-symmetric-encryption: `sdec/2[destructor]`)
  - revealing-signing → `getMessage/1, pk/1, revealSign/2, revealVerify/3,
    true/0`
  - locations-report → `check_rep/2[destructor], get_rep/1[destructor],
    rep/2[private,constructor], report/1`
  - diffie-hellman / bilinear-pairing / multiset / natural-numbers / xor →
    none (their operators/constants — one, inv, DH_neutral, zero, ++, %+ —
    never appear in `functions:`; probes b_diffie-hellman, b_xor, …).
* Layout: `text "functions: " <> fsep (punctuate ',' items)` — fill wrap,
  continuation aligned at column 11 (probe:b_revealing-signing, probe:b_all,
  probe:e_long).

### `equations:` block

* Header `equations:`, or `equations [convergent]:` when the user block is
  declared `[convergent]` (probe:e_conv).
* Base equations always present: `fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1`,
  `snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2` (probe:b_none).
* Per-builtin equation expansions (probes as above):
  - asymmetric-encryption → `adec(aenc(x.1, pk(x.2)), x.2) = x.1`
  - signing → `verify(sign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
  - symmetric-encryption → `sdec(senc(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`
  - revealing-signing → `getMessage(revealSign(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`,
    `revealVerify(revealSign(x.1, x.2), x.1, pk(x.2)) = true`
  - locations-report → `check_rep(rep(x.1, x.2), x.2) = x.1`,
    `get_rep(rep(x.1, x.2)) = x.1`
  - hashing → none
* Sort: STRUCTURAL order on the (lhs, rhs) pair — NOT byte order of the
  rendered text (refuted by target:contract — `checkpcs(xc, xpk, …)` prints
  before `checkpcs(xc, pk(xsk), …)` though 'x' > 'p' — and by target:mesh's
  get_b1/get_b2/aes_cmac groups) and NOT declaration order in either
  direction (probes p_eqA/p_eqB and p_eqF/p_eqF2: reversed sources echo
  identically; contract/mesh source order is the reverse of the echo). The
  pinned comparison, lexicographic (lhs first, rhs breaks lhs ties —
  probes p_eqF/p_eqF2):
  - variables sort BELOW all applications regardless of names
    (probes p_eqC/p_eqC2: `f(zzz, …)` < `f(a0, …)` with a0/0 nullary;
    corpus witnesses contract `xpk` < `pk(xsk)`, mesh `cnf` <
    `aes_cmac(…)`);
  - variable vs variable: name bytes (NOT shortlex — probe:p_eqD `azz` <
    `b`), then index (probe:p_eqH user `x` < builtin `x.1`);
  - application vs application: head name bytes FIRST, then arguments
    left-to-right (contract checkpcs decided at argument 2; head-name byte
    order across groups: mesh `aes_ccm_dec` < `aes_cmac`, refuting
    shortlex); arity never discriminates observably except via `pair`
    (probe:p_eqG refutes arity-first: `pair`/2 < `z1`/1);
  - tuples compare as RIGHT-NESTED binary applications named `pair`
    (probe:p_eqE `g(…)` < `<x, y>`; probe:p_eqG `<x, y>` < `z1(…)`;
    probe:p_eqI `<x, zz>` < `<x, b, c>` — the binary view compares var `zz`
    vs app `pair(b, c)`; a flattened elementwise view would reverse them).
  UNOBSERVABLE ranks (cannot occur in an equations block, flagged): literal
  constants (rejected upstream — probe:e_samehead raw), exp/AC operators,
  diff, pattern-match. Implemented as application-rank under their rendered
  head spelling; corpus gate is the backstop if they ever occur.
* Dedup: EXACT duplicates collapse (probe:e_dup); alpha-equivalent but
  differently-named equations do NOT (probe:e_adedup keeps user
  `fst(<a, b>) = a` alongside the builtin fst equation).
* Layout: `sep (header : map (nest 4) (punctuate ',' eqDocs))` — i.e.
  all-or-nothing, NOT fill:
  - fits on one line (≤ 73) → `equations: e1, e2, e3` (probe:e_mid at
    exactly 73);
  - otherwise header alone, then EVERY equation on its own line at indent 4
    with trailing comma on all but the last — even when several would fit
    joined at indent 4 (probe:e_conv: joined length 71 still one-per-line).
* Each equation is `sep [lhsDoc, nest (-2) (text "= " <> rhsDoc)]`:
  one line `lhs = rhs`; when too long, `= rhs` drops to (equation indent − 2)
  (probe:e_long — `  = xlongvariablename1` at column 2 under equations at
  indent 4).

## Term rendering

Observed atoms:

| AST shape | rendered | provenance |
|---|---|---|
| var, untagged/msg | `x` | everywhere |
| var, fresh | `~x` | target:cav13 (`x:fresh` source → `~x`) |
| var, pub | `$A` | target:cav13 |
| var, node | `#i` | targets (lemma text) |
| var, nat | `%x` | target:NumberSubtermTests |
| var with index k>0 | `name.k` after the sigil (`~x.7`, `x.10`, `XB.10`) | targets: variants + builtin eqs |
| pub literal | `'g'`, `'hello_world'` | probe:t_pair |
| fresh literal | `~'n'` | probe:t_frlit |
| nat literal 1 (`%1`, `1:nat`) | `%1` | probe:t_nat |
| nat literal n (`2:nat`) | `%2` | probe:t_num2 |
| DH one (`1` in DH theory) | `one` | probe:t_one, probe:t_gone |
| DH neutral | `DH_neutral` | probe:t_gone, target:cav13 variant 7 |
| xor zero | `zero` (nullary-app form) | probe:t_xor |

Composite shapes:

* Application: `f(a1, a2)` — `text f <> "(" <> fsep (punctuate ',' args) <>
  ")"`; commas attach to the preceding arg, fill space between args, wrap
  aligns after the `(`, closing `)` attached to the last arg
  (probe:t_wide W2 — continuation line `ylongvariablename2,
  zlongvariablename3)`; probe:e_long). Nullary symbols render bare (`shk`,
  `zero`, `true`, `f` — targets + probes).
  The application `)` is the plain BESIDE `<> ")"`, NOT a droppable fill item:
  when the last argument is a multi-line tuple whose `>` drops to its own line,
  the `)` STAYS JOINED to it as `>)` (probe:appdrop `someop(…, <…, macf(…)>)`
  → `…dd>)`; probe:appdrop2 nested → `…>))`; the round-2 blocker-2 corpus —
  ake/dh UM_three_pass etc. — has MAC applications with this exact shape). This
  is the crux of R4 blocker 2: the enclosing-delimiter drop is operator-
  specific — an AC-operator/pair `)`/`>` DROPS (fill item), an application `)`
  does NOT (beside). Changing `app_doc` to drop the `)` would break these
  witnesses; the fix is to leave it beside.
* Pair: `<a, b, c>` — ONE fill (`fcat`) whose items are `<`, each element
  with its attached `", "` under `nest 1`, and `>`:
  `fcat ('<' : map (nest 1) (punctuate ", " elems) ++ ['>'])`. This single
  construction reproduces every observed shape (probe:p_pw1 wfa–wfd,
  target:mesh k2 equation, probe:t_wide W1):
  - fits → `<a, b>` on one line; a wrapped fill line ends with the trailing
    space of the attached `", "` (probe:t_wide W1 byte-checked);
  - overflow elements continue at (column of `<`) + 1 — the nest 1;
  - when the FIRST element's one-liner does not fit beside `<`, the `<`
    stays ALONE on its line (probe:p_pw1 wfb; mesh k2 inner tuple);
  - `>` sits beside the last element when that element ends a fill line
    (p_pw1 wfa/wfb/wfd), but drops to its own line at the column of `<`
    (no nest) when the last element is multi-line (p_pw1 wfc; mesh k2
    outer tuple) — the fill places items after a multi-line item below it.
  The earlier R1 law (`"<" <> fcat … <> ">"`, `<`/`>` always attached) was
  WRONG — falsified by mesh k2 / p_pw1; it agreed with the true law only on
  the shapes R1 observed (fits, and wfa/wfd-style wraps).
  Right-nested pairs flatten (`<x, <y, z>>` → `<x, y, z>`);
  a pair in NON-last position stays delimited: `<<x, y>, z>` (probe:t_pair).
  Source `pair(x, y)` arrives already normalized to a pair (probe:t_pair).
* Exponentiation: `a^b`, chains render FLAT for both left- and right-nested
  trees: `('g'^~x)^~y` AND `'g'^(~x^~y)` both echo `'g'^~x^~y`
  (probe:t_exp2). No parentheses are ever added around exp or its operands
  (operand classes that need delimiting — AC ops — are self-parenthesizing).
* AC operators mult `*`, xor `⊕` (U+2295), multiset `++`, nat-plus `%+`:
  always wrapped in parens, no spaces, arguments flattened across BOTH sides
  regardless of source nesting: `(x⊕y⊕z)`, `(a++b++c)`, `(%x%+%y%+%z)`,
  `('g'-exponent (~x*~y*~z))` (probes:t_xor, t_uni, t_nat, t_mult2, t_exp2 —
  the AC rule variants normalize (g^x)^y to `'g'^(~x*~y)`).
  The parens are intrinsic: they appear in every context (function argument
  `inv((~x.7*x.11))` — target:cav13 variants; fact argument `Out( (~x⊕~y) )`).
  Layout on overflow: the SAME single-fill construction as tuples, with the
  delimiters as fill items —
  `fcat ('(' : map (nest 1) (punctuate op elems) ++ [')'])`:
  break between elements with the operator attached to the preceding element
  and no fill space, continuation at (column of `(`) + 1 (probe:t_uniwide:
  line ends `…aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa3++`), and the `)` drops to its OWN line at
  the column of `(` when it does not fit beside the last element
  (round-3 target:alethea Universal_VerProofV_v1 — the union keeps both wide
  tuple elements on one 71-column fill line and only `)))"` drops). The
  earlier R1 law (`"(" <> fcat … <> ")"`, `)` always attached) agreed with
  the true construction on every R1-observed shape but was falsified by the
  alethea witness.
  The R4 blocker-2 corpus (ake/dh: UM_three_pass{,_combined,_combined_fixed},
  DHKEA{,_keyreg}) exercises the OTHER `)`-drop sub-case: the LAST union element
  is itself a multi-line tuple, so the tuple's `>` drops to its `<` column AND
  the union's `)` drops BELOW it to the `(` column, on SEPARATE lines
  (probe:uniondrop `(<'1', …>++<'2', dd, macf(…)>)` →
  `…>` / `…)` on their own lines; UM_three_pass echo lines ~54–55, whole-block
  byte-parity re-asserted). The alethea round-3 fix (`)` as a fill item)
  already covers this; the round-2 curated set now re-asserts it across the
  five ake/dh files. (Contrast the application `)` above, which stays `>)`.)
* diff: `diff(x, y)` — application form (probe:t_diff, run with --diff).
* mult inside exp exponent: `x.10^(x.11*inv(x.12))` (target:cav13) — normal
  AC-paren rule, no extra exp parens.

## Rule blocks (R2)

Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
parity). A rule BLOCK is:

```
rule (modulo E) Name[attrs]:
   <body>
<blank line>
  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
  <variants comment>              (col 2)
```

### Header

* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, process="…",
  no_derivcheck, issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < process <
  no_derivcheck < issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST
  color/process/role declaration wins; external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` single-quoted.
  Fill-wrap aligned after the `[`, `]:`  attached to the last item
  (probe:p_rattr R3, target:issue713 bla).
* **SAPIC `process` attribute** (R4 blocker-1 correction; probe:p_process,
  target:ct — the earlier "process is DROPPED" law was WRONG, from a corpus
  that had no `issapicrule` files in the round-2 set). It is present on every
  SAPIC-generated (`issapicrule`) rule, ABSENT otherwise, and renders
  `process="<snippet>"` — DOUBLE quotes (contrast `role='…'`), positioned
  between `color` and `no_derivcheck`. The `<snippet>` is the translated
  process step, carried VERBATIM as one unbreakable text token: `process="|"`
  (parallel), `process="!"` (replication), `process="0"` (null),
  `process="in(x.1);"`, `process="event Ev( x.1 );"`,
  `process="out(<x.1, 'lbl'>);"`, `process="lookup <'att', h.3> as a.1"`,
  `process="insert <$ca.2, 'proofOfID', $s.1, pk(skS.1)>,'yes';"`. Its interior
  spaces, commas, `<>`, `()`, `;` and SINGLE-quoted string constants are part
  of the value, NOT layout — the fill never breaks inside it (target:ct 100+
  witnesses; probe:p_process). ESCAPING of `"`/`\` is UNOBSERVABLE: process
  snippets quote their constants with `'…'`, so no `"`/`\`/control char ever
  reaches the attribute; rendered with no escaping (flagged — a corpus with a
  process containing `"`/`\` would be needed to pin it).

### Body

* `nest 3 (sep [prem-group, nest (-1) arrow-group, concl-group])` — one line
  `[ … ] --[ … ]-> [ … ]` when it fits, otherwise three rows: groups at
  col 3, arrow at col 2. TRUE sep, not a fill: cav13 Step1 keeps `-->` on
  its own row although it would fit beside the premise row.
* Bracket groups (premises, conclusions, AND the action arrow — one
  construction, probe:p_arr1): `sep [sep [open, fsep facts], close]`:
